title: Jackson Mann site could include housing, study finds ahead of community meeting
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Mayor Michelle Wu announced a study indicating Jackson Mann Community Center could include separate housing, alongside its reconstruction.
A community meeting on May 28 will address the site's future, with $10 million allocated for design and permitting.
The site might support mixed-use development, including housing, a new school, and recreational facilities.

title: Here’s how hot meteorologists say it could get in Boston this week
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Boston may break a record high temperature as it is forecasted to reach 95°F on Tuesday.
The National Weather Service predicts HeatRisk reaching a major level Tuesday in cities from the Mid-Atlantic to New England.
Temperatures in Boston are expected to be abnormally high through Wednesday, with potential severe thunderstorms by afternoon.

title: Here’s what Patriots could reportedly net in a Kayshon Boutte trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' Kayshon Boutte is absent from voluntary workouts amid rumored A.J. Brown trade.
ESPN's Mike Reiss suggests Boutte's trade due to overlapping roles with Brown.
Albert Breer hints Boutte could be traded for a fifth- or sixth-round draft pick.

title: Fundraisers launched as 8 remain hospitalized days after fire, explosion at Maine lumber mill
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: A fire and explosion at Robbins Lumber in Searsmont, Maine, killed firefighter Andrew Cross and injured 12 others.
More than $30,000 has been raised for affected first responders, aiming for a $40,000 goal through GoFundMe.
Investigations are underway by Maine's Fire Marshal and ATF, despite challenging conditions, with Robbins Lumber's cooperation.

title: Shams Charania expects Celtics’ pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo to carry into ‘this offseason’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Celtics are reportedly interested in acquiring Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Bucks this offseason.
Milwaukee's willingness to trade Antetokounmpo follows their recent underperformance; several teams are also interested.
Antetokounmpo averaged 30.4 points, 11.9 rebounds, and 6.5 assists per game in the 2024-25 season.

title: T police: Man’s throat slashed in bus station altercation
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A man was taken to the hospital after his throat was slashed during an altercation at Nubian Square bus station.
The incident occurred Friday night at 8:06 p.m. following a verbal argument between two men, according to police.
MBTA Transit Police are investigating and have not confirmed if the alleged attacker has been arrested.

title: Red Sox get discouraging update on Roman Anthony’s injury after he tries swinging bat
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony will be sidelined until Thursday after experiencing soreness while swinging a bat due to a finger injury.
Anthony sprained a ligament in his right ring finger on May 4 and has been limited to 30 games this season.
Despite initial recovery optimism, Anthony's return to the lineup has been delayed, with weekend play against the Twins in doubt.

title: Bill Belichick gets candid on Tom Brady’s growth with Patriots: ‘He wasn’t even really good, but he became great’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bill Belichick discussed Tom Brady's growth from a fourth-string quarterback to a franchise star on 'Pardon My Take'.
Belichick described Brady's early role as a game manager who gradually became the focal point of opponents' game plans.
Belichick, now coaching at UNC, enjoys mentoring young players despite a challenging first season with a 4-8 record.

title: As Minogue pours millions into his own campaign, state lawmaker pushes to ban self-funding
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: GOP candidate Mike Minogue has self-funded $13.5 million for his Massachusetts gubernatorial campaign, the largest individual contribution on record.
State Sen. Rebecca Rausch is proposing an amendment to ban unlimited self-funding in political campaigns, calling it a threat to democracy.
Minogue's primary opponent, Brian Shortsleeve, self-funded $400,000, while Gov. Maura Healey's campaign highlights fundraising disadvantages.

title: Celtics fans voted NBA’s ‘most obnoxious’ in anonymous player poll
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics fans were voted both the 'most passionate' and 'most obnoxious' in an anonymous NBA player poll by The Athletic.
TD Garden narrowly outranked Madison Square Garden for having the most passionate fans, receiving 20.3% of the vote.
TD Garden fans received 32% of the vote for being the most obnoxious, outpacing Philadelphia's Xfinity Mobile arena at 17.9%.
